req.title: Fraud alert response (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-088 covers four distinct behaviours: (1) confirming fraud closes the alert and triggers card reissuance, (2) denying fraud closes the alert without reissuance, (3) duplicate submissions with the same Idempotency-Key return the original response (idempotency), (4) responding to an expired/auto-frozen alert returns HTTP 410. I selected five tests to achieve full coverage of the happy-path EP partitions (confirm vs deny), the idempotency invariant (STC – same state must be re-entered safely), the 24-hour expiry boundary (BVA), and the expired-alert error pathway (NEG). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down alert resources, uses a deterministic idempotency key derived from the test name, and makes no assumptions about pre-existing server state.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in state=active for a known customer account
The customer holds a valid auth token
No prior response has been submitted for this alert
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} and a unique Idempotency-Key header
Inspect the HTTP response status and body
GET /alerts/{id} to verify final alert state
GET /cards?account={account_id} to verify a new card was issued
expected: HTTP 200 with body containing status=closed and fraud_confirmed=true
A new card record exists with issued_reason=fraud_replacement
trace: R-FIN-088: customer confirms suspected fraud -> alert closed, card reissued
tests.executable:
def test_confirm_fraud_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(active_alert, auth_headers, api_client):
    alert_id = active_alert["id"]
    account_id = active_alert["account_id"]
    idem_key = <generate_idempotency_key>("confirm_fraud", alert_id)

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed"
    assert body["fraud_confirmed"] is True

    alert_resp = api_client.get(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert alert_resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

    cards_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/cards", params={"account_id": account_id}, headers=auth_headers
    )
    new_cards = [
        c for c in cards_resp.json()["cards"]
        if c.get("issued_reason") == "fraud_replacement"
    ]
    assert len(new_cards) >= 1

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_closes_alert_without_card_reissuance
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in state=active for a known customer account
The customer holds a valid auth token
No prior response has been submitted for this alert
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} and a unique Idempotency-Key header
Inspect the HTTP response status and body
GET /cards?account={account_id} to confirm no new card was issued
expected: HTTP 200 with body containing status=closed and fraud_confirmed=false
No new card with issued_reason=fraud_replacement exists on the account
trace: R-FIN-088: customer denies suspected fraud -> alert closed, card NOT reissued
tests.executable:
def test_deny_fraud_closes_alert_without_card_reissuance(active_alert, auth_headers, api_client):
    alert_id = active_alert["id"]
    account_id = active_alert["account_id"]
    idem_key = <generate_idempotency_key>("deny_fraud", alert_id)

    cards_before = api_client.get(
        "/cards", params={"account_id": account_id}, headers=auth_headers
    ).json()["cards"]
    card_ids_before = {c["id"] for c in cards_before}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "deny"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed"
    assert body["fraud_confirmed"] is False

    cards_after = api_client.get(
        "/cards", params={"account_id": account_id}, headers=auth_headers
    ).json()["cards"]
    new_fraud_cards = [
        c for c in cards_after
        if c["id"] not in card_ids_before
        and c.get("issued_reason") == "fraud_replacement"
    ]
    assert len(new_fraud_cards) == 0

tests.name: test_duplicate_submission_same_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in state=active for a known customer account
The customer holds a valid auth token
A first PATCH confirm response has already been recorded for a specific Idempotency-Key
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} using Idempotency-Key K (first call)
Record response body and ETag/request-id from first call
PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} using the same Idempotency-Key K (second call)
Compare second response body and status code with first
expected: Response bodies are byte-for-byte identical (or semantically equivalent)
No duplicate card reissuance occurs (card count unchanged between call 1 and call 2)
trace: R-FIN-088: idempotency invariant - duplicate Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_submission_same_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(active_alert, auth_headers, api_client):
    alert_id = active_alert["id"]
    account_id = active_alert["account_id"]
    idem_key = <generate_idempotency_key>("idem_confirm", alert_id)
    payload = {"response": "confirm"}
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    first = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200
    first_body = first.json()

    cards_after_first = api_client.get(
        "/cards", params={"account_id": account_id}, headers=auth_headers
    ).json()["cards"]

    second = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 200
    second_body = second.json()

    assert first_body["status"] == second_body["status"]
    assert first_body["fraud_confirmed"] == second_body["fraud_confirmed"]

    cards_after_second = api_client.get(
        "/cards", params={"account_id": account_id}, headers=auth_headers
    ).json()["cards"]
    replacement_after_first = sum(1 for c in cards_after_first if c.get("issued_reason") == "fraud_replacement")
    replacement_after_second = sum(1 for c in cards_after_second if c.get("issued_reason") == "fraud_replacement")
    assert replacement_after_first == replacement_after_second

tests.name: test_response_at_exactly_24h_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: A fraud alert exists whose created_at timestamp is exactly 24 hours ago (within the SLA window, at the boundary)
The system clock used by the API is controlled or mocked via <freeze_time_fixture>
The customer holds a valid auth token
actions: Set system time to exactly created_at + 23h59m59s (last valid second)
PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} and a unique Idempotency-Key
trace: R-FIN-088: unacknowledged alerts auto-freeze after 24 hours; boundary is last valid moment
tests.executable:
def test_response_at_exactly_24h_boundary_is_accepted(boundary_alert_23h59m59s, auth_headers, api_client):
    alert_id = boundary_alert_23h59m59s["id"]
    idem_key = <generate_idempotency_key>("bva_boundary", alert_id)

    with <freeze_time_fixture>(boundary_alert_23h59m59s["expires_at_minus_1s"]):
        resp = api_client.patch(
            f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
            json={"response": "confirm"},
            headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
        )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_response_to_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert that was created more than 24 hours ago exists and has been auto-frozen (state=expired)
The account associated with the alert has been auto-frozen
The customer holds a valid auth token
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} against an expired alert id
Inspect the HTTP status code and error body
expected: Response body contains error code ALERT_EXPIRED or equivalent machine-readable code
Account remains in frozen state (no state change induced by the late response)
trace: R-FIN-088: error pathway - 410 when alert expired
tests.executable:
def test_response_to_expired_alert_returns_410(expired_alert, auth_headers, api_client):
    alert_id = expired_alert["id"]
    account_id = expired_alert["account_id"]
    idem_key = <generate_idempotency_key>("neg_expired", alert_id)

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert error_body.get("error_code") == "ALERT_EXPIRED"

    account_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert account_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"
